name: World of Aethyros
description:
WORLD OF
AETHYROS
Survive a living fantasy world of magic, monsters, politics, danger, and choice.

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

Step into Aethyros.
A high-fantasy world of ancient ruins, dangerous roads, strange magic, cursed places, political factions, wandering monsters, forgotten gods, and people who may become allies, rivals, lovers, enemies, or something worse.

You are not locked into one role. Be an adventurer, mage, mercenary, noble, criminal, healer, hunter, monster-tamer, cursed survivor, scholar, trader, runaway, priest, spy, or just someone trying not to die in a world that does not care if you are ready.

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

What this bot is built for
Exploration • Combat • Fantasy • Roleplay • Romance • Factions • Quests • Monster encounters • Skill growth • Survival • Politics • Slice-of-life • Dungeon delves • Player-led stories

Available Intros
Intro 1: General Start
Intro 2: Capital of Thalorea Start
Intro 3: Aurelian Empire Start
Intro 4: Monster / Unknown Creature Start
Intro 5: Dwarven Nation Start
Intro 6: Elven Territory Start

Skills & Progression
Aethyros uses a flexible skill-based fantasy structure. Your character can develop talents through action, training, combat, study, magic, aura use, miracles, social choices, survival, crafting, faction work, and other story events.

Monsters & Threats
Expect beasts, spirits, undead, corrupted creatures, intelligent monsters, hostile factions, strange anomalies, cursed relics, dangerous ruins, and enemies that may require more than brute force to survive.

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

How to Steer the Bot
This bot uses a large dynamic lore system. You can guide the pacing and tone during roleplay by speaking directly in chat.

Useful commands / phrases:
“Less lore, more scene.” — Reduces exposition and keeps the reply focused on action.
“Expand the lore.” — Asks for deeper worldbuilding and explanations.
“Slower pacing.” — Makes the scene breathe more and reduces sudden events.
“More combat.” — Pushes the story toward danger and action.
“Less combat.” — Pushes the story toward travel, dialogue, mystery, or politics.
“More politics.” — Brings factions, courts, laws, and intrigue forward.
“More slice-of-life.” — Focuses on food, travel, towns, relationships, culture, and quieter scenes.
creator: Tsevs
